steps.title: Use the intercept condition
steps.content:
A $y$-intercept has an $x$-coordinate of $0$. Since the intercept is $(k-5,b)$:
$$k-5=0$$
$$k=5$$

steps.title: Find the slope
steps.content:
The table gives the points $(k,13)$ and $(k+7,-15)$. Their slope is:
$$m=\frac{-15-13}{(k+7)-k}=\frac{-28}{7}=-4$$

steps.title: Move to the intercept
steps.content:
From $(k,13)$ to $(k-5,b)$, the $x$-value decreases by $5$. With slope $-4$, moving left $5$ raises $y$ by $20$.
$$b=13+20=33$$
The answer is 33.
